phase_evidence.phase_name: Phase 4 difficult fault localization and signal attribution
Phase 6 memory generalization and repository security

validation.semantic_justification: Model provider request did not produce a candidate.

planner.headline: conflict and repository-injection overrides are rejected

security.headline: 8/8 controlled threats handled
security.claim: process-level defense in depth, not a container security boundary

cost_and_latency.headline: Rule, LLM, and Hybrid cost/latency available

v2_v3_repository_startup.reason: V3 adds isolated runtimes and new startup policy; raw counts are context only

v2_v3_fault_localization.reason: V3 uses a real-bug repository-disjoint split and V2 uses a controlled mutation benchmark

v2_v3_patch_repair.reason: V2 LLM metrics use deterministic fixtures while V3 uses a completed 120-trial live-model real-bug protocol; no uplift is calculated

v2_v3_memory.reason: V3 changes authority, conflict, stale-scope, and advisory-memory gates

skip_explanations: tests/test_runtime_security.py cannot create a symbolic link on this Windows host.
tests/test_v3_semantic_validation.py cannot create a symbolic link on this Windows host.
latest_regression.skip_explanation: The Windows host did not permit two unit tests to create symbolic links. Deterministic traversal rejection and runtime workspace symlink scans remain covered.

validation.semantic_justification: Model provider request did not produce a candidate.

claim_boundaries: Offline Phase 0-6 evidence is combined with a validated 120-trial live LLM/Hybrid evaluation; neither substitutes for the other.
Rule metrics, human-fix semantic calibration, and deterministic memory/security fixtures are not live-model repair rates.
V2/V3 numbers are not presented as improvements when their protocols differ.
A complete V3 release retains all failed trials, provider blockers, environment blockers, token usage, cost, latency, and generator attribution in the denominator.
Provider-access preflight overhead is reported separately and never counted as a repair Trial or pass@k success.
Process-level repository defenses do not provide container-grade isolation for native child processes on Windows.
